Tally vs 1Password: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Tally and 1Password? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Tally if you need freelancers or small-teams. Choose 1Password if you prioritize families or teams.

Tally

PricingFree / $29/mo Pro
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Document-style form builder with clean, professional design
  • Conditional logic and branching for dynamic forms
  • Unlimited forms and responses on free tier
  • Embedding, popup, and link sharing options
  • 800+ integrations via Zapier, Make, and native connections
  • Collaboration features for team form building

Pros

  • Forms look like real documents, not templates; the design is clean and professional by default
  • Free tier is seriously generous — unlimited forms, unlimited responses, no watermark
  • Logic jumps and conditional fields work well for complex forms and surveys
  • Integrations with Notion, Slack, Google Sheets, and 500+ tools via Zapier/Make
  • You can embed forms anywhere or use Tally's hosted pages (which look great on mobile)

Cons

  • Design customization is limited compared to Typeform or Jotform; you get Tally's look and that's it
  • No native payment collection built in; you'll need to embed Stripe or PayPal separately
  • File uploads on free tier are limited to 10MB; Pro bumps it to 100MB
  • No native analytics beyond basic response tracking
  • The Pro plan ($29/mo) is pricey for what is essentially a form builder

1Password

Pricing$2.99/mo Individual / $4.99/mo Families / $7.99/mo Business
Rating★★★★½ 4.6/5

Key Features

  • Cross-platform password manager with polished UX
  • Family vaults for shared household passwords
  • Travel Mode for removing vaults at borders
  • Watchtower security monitoring and alerts
  • SSO and provisioning for business deployments
  • Virtual payment cards (US only) for online purchases

Pros

  • User experience is best-in-class; the apps are beautifully designed and a pleasure to use on every platform
  • Watchtower security dashboard alerts you to compromised passwords, weak passwords, and two-factor issues
  • Family sharing is excellent — shared vaults for household logins without exposing personal passwords
  • Travel Mode lets you remove sensitive vaults from your devices when crossing borders
  • Desktop app works offline and syncs when connected, so you're never locked out

Cons

  • Subscription-only — no lifetime license option; you pay $2.99/mo forever or lose access
  • Not open-source, which is a dealbreaker for some security-conscious users
  • Browser extension quality varies between browsers; the Chrome extension is best, others lag
  • Windows app is good but clearly gets less attention than the Mac version
  • Business tier pricing ($7.99/seat/mo) is expensive compared to alternatives
Try 1Password →

Which Should You Choose?

Both Tally and 1Password are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Tally if: Tally is the form builder that looks like a document. Instead of the clunky drag-and-drop builders that produce obviously templated forms, Tally lets you write your form like you're writing a page — with real text, headings, and dividers between form fields. It's a small idea that makes a huge difference in how professional your forms look. And the free tier is genuinely generous.
  • Choose 1Password if: 1Password is the password manager that feels like a native Mac app — polished, thoughtfully designed, and a pleasure to use. It's not open-source like Bitwarden, but it compensates with a better user experience, excellent family sharing features, and integrations that just work. If you're willing to pay for a premium experience, 1Password is the gold standard.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
